results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL OF THIS PROJECT IS TO ASSESS THE RELATIONSHIPS BETWEEN DIVERSE CLIMATE-INDUCED CONCERNS RELATED TO WATER INFRASTRUCTURE AND WATER QUALITY AND THEIR EFFECTS ON HEALTH IN ALASKA NATIVE COMMUNITIES. THE PROJECT AIMS TO BUILD CAPACITY AGAINST CLIMATE CHANGE RISKS BY ADDRESSING WATER INFRASTRUCTURE ISSUES IN ALASKA NATIVE COMMUNITIES.ACTIVITIES:THE PROJECT WILL 1) CONDUCT COMMUNITY-BASED MONITORING (CBM) AND DIRECT SAMPLING TO ASSESS WATER QUALITY; 2) GATHER SURVEYS AND INTERVIEWS AND PHOTOVOICE SUBMISSIONS TO CAPTURE WATER INSECURITY AND LOCAL VULNERABILITIES; 3) ASSESS THE CUMULATIVE EFFECTS OF WATER QUALITY, WATER INSECURITY, AND LOCAL VULNERABILITIES ON HEALTH, MENTAL HEALTH AND NON-WESTERN PERSPECTIVE OF HEALTH USING STATISTICAL ANALYSES AND FUZZY-SET QUALITATIVE COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS; AND 4) BUILD RESILIENT WATER INFRASTRUCTURE CAPACITY.SUBRECIPIENT:UNIVERSITY OF TEXAS ($303,681) WILL IDENTIFY TECHNOLOGY AND TECHNIQUES FOR REDUCING CLIMATE-BASED RISKS TO WATER INFRASTRUCTURE IDENTIFIED IN TASK 1.1. WATER INFRASTRUCTURE AND TASK 3.1 BUILD ADAPTIVE CAPACITY OF WATER INFRASTRUCTURE.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL RESEARCH REPORTS AS WELL AS (1) HYDRAULIC MODELS OF THE WATER DISTRIBUTION SYSTEM AND OPEN-SOURCE DATA-DRIVEN ALGORITHMS AND CODES FOR LEAK DETECTION AND LOCALIZATION, (2) MAPS OF THE MOST VULNERABLE HOUSEHOLDS AT RISK OF WATER QUALITY AND INSECURITY, AND (3) COMPREHENSIVE MODELS SHOWING THE FACTORS THAT ARE THE MOST SIGNIFICANT IN DETERMINING HEALTH, MENTAL HEALTH AND NON-WESTERN HEALTH OUTCOMES. EXPECTED OUTCOMES ARE RESILIENT WATER INFRASTRUCTURES AND IMPROVED HEALTH IN ALASKA NATIVE COMMUNITIES. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES INCLUDE TRIBAL COMMUNITIES, AS WELL AS STATE AND LOCAL PUBLIC HEALTH AND ENVIRONMENTAL RESOURCE MANAGERS, IN ALASKA.
